birthPlace.address: Meulan, Yvelines, Ile De France, France

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Contre sceau Robert de Beaumont Autrefois appendu à un abandon par Robert de Beaumont de certains droits qu’il possède à Authon (Sources : Archives départementales Eure-et-Loir, G 2086 (chapitre Notre-Dame de Chartres).
“Royal Ancestry: A Study in Colonial & Medieval Families,” Douglas Richardson (2013): 
  “ROBERT [II], Count of Meulan, son and heir of Waleran (or Galeran) [II], Count of Meulan, Earl of Worcester, seigneur of Beaumont-le-Roger, Brionne, la Croix Saint-Leufroy, Elbeuf, Port-Audemer, etc., by Agnes, daughter of Amaury de Montfort, Count of Evreux. He married about 1165 MAUD OF CORNWALL, daughter of Reynold Fitz Roy, Earl of Cornwall (illegitimate son of King Henry I), by Mabel, daughter of William Fitz Richard [see ENGLAND 2.i for her ancestry]. They had three sons, Waleran (or Galeran), Peter [Dean of Wimborne], and Henry, and two daughters, Mabel and Jeanne (wife of Guy IV, seigneur of La Roche Guyon). In 1173 he adhered to the young King Henry in his revolt against Henry II, but was reconciled to the king. Holding lands in both France and Normandy, the conflict between Philip Augustus and the English kings placed him in a difficult position, and eventually brought about his ruin. In 1188 when Philip took Vedôme, he captured 62 knights sent by the Count of Meulan to assist the defence. In 1191 he was excommunicated for taking action against William de Longchamp, Bishop of Ely. In 1202 he confirmed the charter of his sister, Isabel de Mayenne, to the monks of Savigny. In 1202 he revolted against King Philippe Auguste of France, and suffered the confiscation of his county of Meulan in consequence. Conversely, he had incurred King John's malevolentia before 2 April 1203, when the abbot of Saint-Taurin d'Evreux acted as an intermediary to reconcile him with the king of England. In a vain attempt to save his position, he handed over all of his estates 1 May 1204 to  his daughter, Mabel, and her husband, William de Vernon, Earl of Devon. John, however, seized his English estates, and Philippe those in France and Normandy. ROBERT, deposed Count of Meulan, was living 14 November 1207; he died 16 August, about 1212. His widow, Maud, was living 25 October 1212; she reportedly died 1220-1. 
****************************

Our royal, titled, noble and commoner ancestors

Sir Robert II de Beaumont, Count de Meulan1,2,3
Last Edited	4 Apr 2020
M, #13011, d. circa 16 August 1212

Father	Waleran de Beaumont, Comte de Meulan, Earl of Worcester, Seigneur de Beaumont-le-Roger, Brionne, la Crois St.-Leufroy, Elbeuf, Port-Audemer, & Vatteville4 b. c 1104, d. 10 Apr 1166
Mother	Agnes de Montfort4 d. 15 Dec 1181
     
Sir Robert II de Beaumont, Count de Meulan Isenburg, chart 702, Vol. 3, excludes this generation. 

He married Maud Fitz-Roy, daughter of Reginald de Dunstanville FitzRoy (FitzHenry), Earl of Cornwall, Sheriff of Devon and Mabel FitzWilliam, circa 1165; 

They had 3 sons (Waleran (Galeran); Peter, Dean of Wimborne; & Henry) & 2 daughters (Mabel, wife of William de Vernon, 5th Earl of Devon; &Jeanne, wife of Guy IV, Seigneur de la Roche-Guyon).3 Sir Robert II de Beaumont, Count de Meulan died circa 16 August 1212.3

Family
Maud Fitz-Roy d. bt 25 Oct 1212 - 1221

Children:  3 sons, 2 daughters

Jeanne de Meullent+

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Beaumont於大約1142年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯易維林斯姆奧藍--易維林斯時，他的父親Galeran Valeran de Beaumont39歲，母親Elizabeth De Montfort21歲。他於大約1160年與Maud de Dunstanville在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1204年8月16日在法國努維勒-阿吉泰維安波爾斯享年63歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯普雷奧。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Beaumont was born about 1142, in Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, his father, Galeran Valeran de Beaumont, was 39 and his mother, Elizabeth De Montfort, was 21. He married Maud de Dunstanville about 1160, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 16 August 1204, in Poitiers, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, at the age of 63, and was buried in Préaux, Calvados, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Beaumont est né environ 1142, à Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, France, son père, Galeran Valeran de Beaumont, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth De Montfort, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Dunstanville environ 1160, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 16 août 1204, à Poitiers, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Préaux, Calvados, Normandie, France.

ko.Préaux, Mayenne, Pays de la Loire, Franc: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 메이니 플e오스
ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Beaumont(이)가 대략 1142년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 이벨라이네스 우룰아--이벨라이네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Galeran Valeran de Beaumont(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth De Montfort(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1160년 영국 데폰에서 Maud de Dunstanville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1204년 8월 16일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 비니 포이티어스에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 캘바도스 플e오스에 묻혔다.

pt.Préaux, Mayenne, Pays de la Loire, Franc: Préaux, Mayenne, Pays de la Loire, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Beaumont nasceu aproximadamente 1142, em Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Galeran Valeran de Beaumont, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth De Montfort, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Dunstanville aproximadamente 1160, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 16 de agosto de 1204, em Poitiers, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Préaux, Calvados, Normandie, França.

ru.Préaux, Mayenne, Pays de la Loire, Franc: прeаукс, меиенн, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Beaumont родился около 1142 г. в местности меулан-ен-ивелаинесь, ивелаинесь, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, его отцу по имени Galeran Valeran de Beaumont было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth De Montfort, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Dunstanville около 1160 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер 16 августа 1204 г. в местности поитиирс, виенн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности прeаукс, калвадос, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Beaumont nació aproximadamente 1142, en Meulan-en-Yvelines, Yvelines, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Galeran Valeran de Beaumont, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Elizabeth De Montfort, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Maud de Dunstanville aproximadamente 1160, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 16 de agosto de 1204, en Poitiers, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Préaux, Calvados, Normandie, Francia.
